samples.text: If the plant receives sunlight, then the plant grows. It is not the case that the plant receives sunlight. Does it follow that the plant grows?
Whenever the plant receives sunlight, the plant grows. However, in this situation it is not true that the plant receives sunlight. Can we conclude that the plant grows?
If the plant gets sunshine, then the plant develops. It is not the case that the plant gets sunshine. Does it follow that the plant develops?
The only stated cause of 'the plant grows' is 'the plant receives sunlight'. That cause did not occur. Can we conclude that the plant grows?
If the plant receives sunlight, then the plant grows. It is not the case that the plant receives sunlight. A blue car is parked outside. Does it follow that the plant grows?
